failedAssertions.reason: Error UI not displayed when API is unreachable. hasErrorUI() returns false because the app doesn't show error toasts when store API calls fail (profile load, session create, etc.).
Error UI not displayed for 500 errors on session create, profile load, and chat API. The stores don't trigger error toasts for these specific 500 scenarios.
Timeout error UI not displayed for specific timeout scenarios. The stores don't show error toasts when requests timeout.

systemicIssues.description: Stores don't show error toasts when API calls fail (500, network error, timeout). profileStore.loadProfile fails silently, sessionStore.beginSession sets error but MeditationChat doesn't display it, settings store doesn't show errors.
systemicIssues.impact: VAL-ERROR-001, VAL-ERROR-002, VAL-ERROR-003 - 5 specific test scenarios fail because error UI is not displayed when store API calls fail
systemicIssues.rootCause: The hasErrorUI() helper checks for .toast--error, .error-boundary, .meditation-chat__error[role=alert], .session-history__error[role=alert], [role=alert]. These elements are not rendered because stores don't call toast.showError() when API calls fail

reRunNotes: Re-run after fix 'add-error-toasts-to-stores' was created. The fix attempted to add error toasts to stores but the bug persists - same 5 test scenarios fail. The issue is that profileStore.loadProfile, sessionStore.beginSession, and similar store methods don't show error toasts when their API calls fail.
